data.title: Veeva Systems Price Target Maintained With a $355.00/Share by Needham
New Fortress Energy Inc. SEC 10-Q Report
data.description:
New Fortress Energy Inc., a global energy infrastructure company, has released its Form 10-Q report for the third quarter of 2025. The report highlights significant financial and operational metrics, reflecting the company's performance in the energy sector, particularly focusing on LNG and natural gas sales, power generation, and marine operations.

Financial Highlights

Total Revenues: $327.4 million, reflecting a decrease from $567.5 million in the same period last year, primarily due to lower operating revenue and other revenue.

Operating (Loss) Income: $(103.6) million, compared to an income of $79.5 million in the prior year, driven by increased transaction and integration costs and asset impairment expenses.

Net (Loss) Income: $(293.4) million, a significant decline from a net income of $11.3 million in the previous year, impacted by goodwill impairment and higher interest expenses.

Net (Loss) Income Attributable to Common Stockholders: $(299.97) million, compared to $8.1 million last year, reflecting the overall decline in net income.

Net (Loss) Income Per Share – Basic: $(1.07), down from $0.04 in the prior year, due to the substantial net loss incurred during the period.

Net (Loss) Income Per Share – Diluted: $(1.07), compared to $0.03 last year, consistent with the basic earnings per share due to the net loss.

Business Highlights

Revenue Segments: The company operates through two main segments: Terminals and Infrastructure, and Ships. The Terminals and Infrastructure segment includes operations related to LNG and natural gas sales, as well as power generation facilities. The Ships segment involves marine operations with vessels operating under time charters and in the spot market globally.

Geographical Performance: The company has significant operations in the United States, Brazil, and Mexico, with marine operations extending globally. The sale of the Jamaica Business, including LNG import terminals and power plants, was completed, impacting the company's operations in the Caribbean region.

Sales Units: The company reported LNG cargo sales of $207,035 for the nine months ended September 30, 2025, with no LNG cargo sale revenue recognized in the third quarter of 2025.

New Production Launches: The company is engaged in significant development activities in Latin America, including the construction of power plants in Brazil. The successful completion of these projects is subject to various risks, such as obtaining government approvals and securing financing.

Future Outlook: The company is facing challenges related to operating losses and negative cash flows, with a focus on strategic alternatives to improve liquidity. This includes potential asset sales, capital raising, and debt restructuring to address financial constraints and support ongoing operations.

SEC Filing: New Fortress Energy Inc. [ NFE ] - 10-Q - Nov. 20, 2025

data.title: MESA AIR GROUP INC SEC 10-Q Report
data.description:
Mesa Air Group Inc., a regional airline operating under the Capacity Purchase Agreement (CPA) with United Airlines, has released its latest 10-Q report, highlighting significant improvements in financial performance and operational efficiency. The report covers the company's financial and business activities for the most recent quarter, providing insights into its revenue streams, cost management, and future outlook.

Financial Highlights

  • Total Operating Revenues: $90.7 million, a decrease of 21.3% compared to the same period last year due to fewer aircraft under contract.
  • Operating Loss: $9.2 million, an improvement from a loss of $17.0 million in the prior year period, driven by decreased pilot training costs, lower pilot wages, and reduced depreciation expenses.
  • Net Loss: $14.1 million, an improvement from a net loss of $24.9 million in the prior year period, reflecting cost reductions and efficiency improvements.
  • Net Loss Per Share: $(0.34), an improvement from $(0.60) in the prior year period, indicating a reduction in losses per share.

Business Highlights

  • Revenue Segments: The company's operating revenues are primarily derived from contract revenue under the Capacity Purchase Agreement (CPA) with United Airlines, which includes fixed monthly amounts per aircraft, additional amounts based on flights and block hours flown, and pass-through revenue for certain direct expenses.
  • Sales Units: As of September 30, 2025, Mesa operated a fleet of 60 Embraer 175 regional aircraft with approximately 234 daily departures, indicating a stable operational capacity in terms of fleet size and daily operations.
  • Operational Performance: The company reported a decrease in flight operations expenses due to reduced pilot training and lower pilot wages, contributing to improved operational efficiency.
  • Fleet Management: The company completed the sale of several aircraft, which contributed to a decrease in depreciation expenses and impairment charges related to held-for-sale assets.
  • Future Outlook: The company anticipates continued operations under the United CPA, with a focus on maintaining operational efficiency and managing costs effectively. The merger with Republic Airways Holdings, Inc. is expected to enhance the company's financial position and operational capabilities.

SEC Filing: MESA AIR GROUP INC [ MESA ] - 10-Q - Nov. 20, 2025

data.title: The 10-Point: The Wall Street Journal's Guide to the Day's Top News
data.description:
By Emma Tucker

Wall Street's optimism proved short-lived. Blockbuster results from artificial-intelligence bellwether Nvidia sparked a furious rally early yesterday before indexes reversed course and tumbled in the largest blown gain since April's tariff turmoil. Adding to investors' worries: A shutdown-delayed jobs report did little to clarify the labor market's state or the potential for a December interest-rate cut. The economic whiplash isn't confined to Wall Street: America's middle class is weary and hunting for bargains, still feeling pinched by rising costs of everything from a steak to a new couch after nearly five years of high prices.

Today's Headlines

Stocks surrendered gains made after Nvidia's strong results and closed sharply lower as investors remained jittery over swollen valuations of AI companies.

The planned $20 billion bailout for Argentina from JPMorgan Chase, Bank of America and Citigroup has been shelved as bankers pivot instead to a smaller, short-term, loan package.

Lisa Su, CEO of chip designer AMD, has a new goal: to grab a big chunk of an AI business that could reach $1 trillion a year.

The Transportation Department said it would pay $10,000 bonuses to air-traffic controllers who didn't miss work during the shutdown.

The U.S. is set to designate the "Cartel of the Suns," a loose-knit group of Venezuelan generals and senior officials, as a foreign terrorist organization over its alleged role in enabling the shipment of cocaine to the U.S. and Europe.

Live From The Markets

Paramount, Comcast and Netflix have submitted bids for Warner Bros. Discovery.

The recent comedown in mortgage rates has led to a pop in the number of people looking to refinance home loans. Surprised bond investors might demand a higher yield for the added risk, potentially raising mortgage rates, writes Telis Demos.

Read It Here First

The middle class is buckling under almost five years of persistent inflation.

Perpetual sticker shock is making many middle-class Americans feel worse about their finances and the future of the country. They are spending more carefully and finding creative ways to save money, report Rachel Louise Ensign and Rachel Wolfe. Teri Kopp and her husband Bill, for instance, often sit in the dark with only strings of LED lights to lower their electric bill. This gloomy outlook stands in stark contrast with the most affluent families, who have been enjoying stock-market gains and powering the economy with their spending.

How the 'smartest guy in the room' got toppled by Epstein emails.

The unraveling of Larry Summers's professional life was driven by the release of messages in which he asked advice from Jeffrey Epstein on "getting horizontal" with someone he described as a mentee. Though the former Treasury secretary's enormous network kept him immune to past Epstein revelations, this time is different: Summers has resigned from the OpenAI board and taken leave from his teaching duties at Harvard University, report Joshua Chaffin, Melissa Korn and Justin Lahart.

Expert Take

Q: What does the delayed September jobs report reveal about the state of the job market and hiring?

The U.S. labor market defied expectations in September, adding 119,000 jobs. At the same time, the unemployment rate rose slightly to 4.4%, the highest level in four years. Columnist Callum Borchers explains what the report can tell us about where the labor market stands, and where it may be headed.

A: In short, not much. This is partly because the delayed release makes the report feel like old news. The bigger issue is we don't know how much faith to put in this preliminary estimate of 119,000 jobs added in September.

It's true that the job-growth figure exceeded many economists' expectations. It's also true that this report included downward revisions to the numbers from July and August.

The Bureau of Labor Statistics now says the economy added 33,000 fewer jobs in those two months than previously thought-including a net loss in August. This is after a different BLS revision in September slashed a 12-month job-growth tally in half.

None of this means there is something nefarious going on. But it is worth remembering that an accurate picture of the job market takes time to develop — and lately, the picture has come to look worse as it ages.

See The Story

See how this tiny island nation became a flashpoint in the Trump-Maduro showdown.

Trinidad and Tobago's government was quick to support the U.S. as President Trump increased pressure on Venezuela and its leader Nicolás Maduro. WSJ's Shelby Holliday reports on how this island nation came to play a significant role in the U.S.'s Caribbean campaign.

Happening Today

Trump is expected to meet with New York mayor-elect Zohran Mamdani at the White House.

Economic data: A final November consumer-sentiment reading is due from the University of Michigan at 10 a.m. A preliminary readout indicated sentiment has fallen toward record-low levels. Also due: a delayed BLS report on inflation-adjusted earnings.

Earnings: BJ's Wholesale

Number Of The Day:

$54.7 million

The record-setting price at which Frida Kahlo's sleeping self-portrait, "The Dream (The Bed)," sold at a Sotheby's auction last night. The 1940 canvas now ranks as the most expensive piece of art by a female artist sold at auction, topping Georgia O'Keeffe's "Jimson Weed/White Flower No. 1," that held the record for 11 years.

And Finally...

​​This year's Thanksgiving surprise: Half of the guests are stoned.

Every Thanksgiving, there's an unspoken ritual that has nothing to do with turkey — it's the cousin walk, an annual marijuana-laced stroll and bonding session for younger family members before dinner. The pre-turkey toke is now big enough to spur sales on what's now known as "Green Wednesday," a marijuana-buying version of Black Friday the day before Thanksgiving. Dispensaries say it's often the second-biggest day for cannabis sales after the pot-celebration day 420 on April 20.

data.title: Street View: Tech push and value play to keep Walmart ahead
data.description:
** Retail giant Walmart lifted its full-year outlook for the second time in 2025 after posting another strong quarter, driven by robust growth in online sales—a sign of confidence as the holiday season approaches

** Co will shift its listing to the Nasdaq from NYSE on Dec. 9, highlighting growing role of technology in its strategy

** Median PT of 44 brokerages covering the stock is $120 - data compiled by LSEG

BRIGHT WALMART FUTURE IN SIGHT

** J.P. Morgan ("overweight," PT: $129) says margin outlook positive, though timing hinges on cost pressures and tariffs; expects gains from new profit streams and sees NASDAQ shift as smart positioning move

** Jefferies ("buy," PT: $125) says WMT well-positioned to gain share as cost-conscious shoppers trade down, while tech and AI investments to boost efficiency

** Telsey Advisory Group ("outperform," PT: $130) expects co to remain a retail leader, supported by value-focused strategy, tech investments and plans to expand into higher-margin businesses like advertising and delivery

** Piper Sandler ("overweight," PT: $123) expects co' s strong value and convenience proposition to drive market share gains

data.title: Haemonetics Price Target Maintained With a $88.00/Share by BTIG
Kenya's $9 Billion Telecom Giant Just Drew a Surprise Bid -- And Vodacom Wants More
data.description:
Kenya's telecom giant just stepped back into the center of the investment conversation, and the narrative could be shifting fast. People familiar with the situation say Vodacom Group Ltd. (VODAF) is quietly exploring a bigger move into Safaricom, potentially buying part of the Kenyan government's stake in a company now valued at 1.19 trillion shillings. Vodacom already owns 39.93% of the business, and while nothing has been finalized, the discussions suggest the company may be weighing a deeper long-term position in East Africa's most valuable listed firm. Vodacom and Safaricom declined to comment, and Kenya's Treasury wasn't immediately available.

The timing matters. Kenya is navigating rising debt costs and budget pressures, and selling a slice of its 35% Safaricom holding could be one of the few levers available to raise revenue without disrupting broader economic plans. Safaricom controls close to two thirds of the country's mobile subscribers, its shares have doubled over the past year, and its expansion into Ethiopia adds another layer of possible upside. The Treasury had previously floated splitting Safaricom into three units, a move that would have reduced the state's stake, though Vodacom has ruled out any idea of spinning off M-Pesa, according to earlier reporting.

For investors, the story sits at the intersection of fintech scale and strategic ambition. Safaricom and Vodacom are already pushing to grow M-Pesa, Africa's largest and fastest-expanding mobile money platform, and a larger ownership position could be viewed as Vodacom strengthening its hold on one of the continent's most powerful digital ecosystems. Vodacom last increased its Safaricom stake through a 2017 all-share deal with Vodafone Plc, and the current talks could be interpreted as another step in that trajectory. While the path forward isn't locked in, the combination of a cash-pressed government and a partner eager to deepen its exposure to a high-growth mobile money engine may set up a pivotal moment in East Africa's telecom and financial landscape.

data.title: Canada futures slip as weak retail forecast and fed rate uncertainty cloud outlook
data.description:
Futures for Canada's main stock index edged lower on Friday, joining a global selloff as investors offloaded risk assets while the U.S. jobs report offered little clarity on interest rate decisions.

Futures on the S&P/TSX Composite Index were down 0.2% as of 05:25 a.m. ET, joining the S&P and Nasdaq futures , which were also lower.

On Thursday, the underlying benchmark reversed early gains to end at a two-week low, joining a selloff at Wall Street despite Nvidia's blockbuster earnings while investors continued to fret about increased technology valuations and AI spending.

The index was on course for its weakest weekly performance since early October.

Canada's retail sales report, due later in the day, is expected to show a 0.7% drop for September, a sign of cooling consumer demand as recent data showed inflation eased to 2.2% in October, offering fresh clues on the health of household spending.

Mixed U.S. jobs data, featuring stronger-than-expected September hiring alongside rising unemployment and downward revisions, has clouded the outlook for the Federal Reserve's next policy decision as officials grow increasingly hawkish.

Markets, especially Wall Street has had a stellar run this year mainly backed by the AI and tech rally and the optimism on easing of borrowing rates. Canadian equities have also moved in tandem with the U.S markets this year often shrugging off economic and financial decisions happened domestically.

Weighing sentiment this morning, gold prices fell nearly 1% as hopes of a U.S rate diminished.

Oil prices fell more than 1% extending declines as a peace deal for Russia-Ukraine war was touted that could boost global supply.

In corporate news, Dye & Durham has been offered $272 million by Plantro to be taken private, Bloomberg News reported.

GFL Environmental announced secondary offering by selling shareholders.

data.title: Radio Free Europe closing in key Trump ally Hungary
data.description:
Key points:
  • RFE/RL to end Hungarian service after relaunch
  • Service restarted in 2020 after 'decline' in media voices
  • Trump aide: RFE/RSL undermined US foreign policy to back Hungary's prime minister
  • Nationalist PM Orban is key Trump supporter in Europe
  • Critics: Orban has tightened control of media while in power

By Anita Komuves

U.S.-funded Radio Free Europe/Radio Liberty (RFE/RL) will shut down in Hungary on Friday, it said, ending a five-year run of a media service that a U.S. official said had damaged President Donald Trump's foreign policy in a key ally.

Launched originally in 1950 to broadcast to communist-run countries during the Cold War, RFE/RL still reaches millions in eastern Europe, including Russia and Ukraine, as well as central Asia and the Middle East, aiming to support media freedom.

But the news service has been hit hard by efforts to downsize government under Trump, who counts Hungarian Prime Minister Viktor Orban, a right-wing nationalist, as a close ally in the European Union.

Kari Lake, Trump's administrator in the U.S. Agency for Global Media that oversees RFE/RL, said this month that taxpayer-funded programming should not be used to "destabilize" Hungary.

In a November 5 letter to Congress, re-posted by Lake on the X social media site, she said funding for RFE/RL would end and that its "programming has undermined President Trump's foreign policy by opposing the duly elected Prime Minister of Hungary."

ORBAN'S TIGHTENING GRIP OVER MEDIA

Hungary was one of only three EU member states that RFE/RL operated in, alongside Romania and Bulgaria.

In a statement late on Thursday, RFE/RL said its team had "worked with dedication to provide independent journalism for audiences in Hungary".

Hungary transitioned to democracy from communism in 1989 like other countries in central Europe that are now EU members. While RFE/RL's Hungarian service ended in 1993, it was relaunched in 2020 due to what it called "a decline in diverse media voices" there.

Critics have accused Orban of tightening media control during the more than 15 years he has been in power. Public media have come under full government control and several private outlets have been shut or taken over by government-friendly owners. Orban has denied stifling media freedoms.

RFE/RL's Hungarian service Szabad Európa, an online news site that also produced video reports and podcasts, reported on government efforts in the courts and justice system, and won a 2024 legal battle to gain access to state highway contracts.

TOUGH ELECTION AHEAD

Orban trails the new centre-right opposition party Tisza in polls before an election next year.

The Hungarian leader met Trump at the White House on November 7, where he secured a one-year reprieve from U.S. sanctions on Russian oil and gas on which Hungary relies.

Orban was also granted a potential U.S. financial shield as an alternative to EU funding frozen due to Budapest's disputes with Brussels over issues including corruption, rule of law and press freedom.

"The Globalists are more than welcome to hate our ally Viktor Orban," Lake posted on X on the day of the meeting.

"What they are not entitled to is the use of YOUR money to destabilize the Hungarian regime via taxpayer-funded programming on Szabad Európa. We're putting a stop to that."

The global Reporters Without Borders press freedom group called the RFE/RL shutdown in Hungary a "serious blow to Hungarians’ right to information" before the 2026 election.

data.title: British Airways' Parent IAG Joins Race to Bid for Stake in Portugal's TAP
data.description:
By Aimee Look

British Airways' parent company IAG said it aims to join the bidding process for a stake in Portugal's TAP, as it contends with Air France-KLM and Deutsche Lufthansa for a slice of the state-owned carrier with coveted routes bridging Europe and Latin America.

International Consolidated Airlines Group--which houses carriers like British Airways, Iberia and Vueling--said Friday that it had submitted its interest to state-holding company Parpublica, seeking to join TAP Air Portugal's privatization. IAG didn't disclose financial details.

"Several terms would need to be addressed before IAG could propose an investment," the group added.

IAG's submission comes after Air France-KLM and Lufthansa said earlier this week they had put forth statements formally expressing interest in purchasing a stake in TAP.

In July, Portugal's government said it would shed a 49.9% stake in TAP, reserving 44.9% for private investors and 5% for company employees. Officials have been mulling over a partial sale of the national carrier for years.

One of the terms of the sale requires buyers to keep the main hub for TAP in Lisbon. IAG said its decentralized model aligns with the Portuguese government's intent to protect TAP, adding that it would have significant potential within the group.

TAP could be a linchpin for routes linking Europe and Latin America, prized for its access to the Latin American market, both Air France-KLM and Lufthansa have said.

Write to Aimee Look at aimee.look@wsj.com

data.title: India's home-built Tejas fighter jet crashes at Dubai air show
data.description:
India's home-produced Tejas fighter jet crashed at the Dubai Air Show on Friday, an Indian Air Force spokesperson said.

A witness said the fighter jet was flying at a low altitude at around 2:15 p.m. (1015 GMT) before coming down in ball of fire. Footage from the site showed black smoke rising from the crash site.

This is the second known crash of the fighter jet, which is powered by General Electric engines. The first crash was during an exercise in India in 2024.

data.title: Gold falls 1%, poised for weekly loss as US jobs data dims rate-cut hopes
data.description:
Key points:
  • Silver prices fall over 3%
  • US posts solid job growth in September
  • Fed's Hammack says further rate cuts could pose risks to economy

By Noel John

Gold prices fell more than 1% on Friday and were set for a weekly decline after a robust U.S. jobs report dampened expectations of a Federal Reserve rate cut next month, weighing on the non-yielding metal.

Spot gold fell 1% to $4,036.21 per ounce, as of 1056 GMT. Bullion has dipped 1% this week.

U.S. gold futures for December delivery fell 0.7% to $4,033.30 per ounce.

"The prospect of further rate cuts has been somewhat doomed by decent labour market data that came out yesterday. I think that's really the primary factor" weighing on gold, said Nitesh Shah, commodities strategist at WisdomTree.

MIXED VIEW OF US LABOUR MARKET

Thursday's delayed U.S. jobs report offered a mixed view of the labour market, with non-farm payrolls increasing by 119,000 jobs, compared with estimates of 50,000, but the jobless rate hitting a four-year high.

The next jobs report is due only after the Fed's December meeting, for which traders now see a 33% chance of a rate cut, down from 44% last week. (FEDWATCH)

Gold, a non-yielding asset, tends to do well in low-interest-rate environments.

Cleveland Fed President Beth Hammack, who opposed the Fed's most recent rate cut, on Thursday cautioned against lowering borrowing costs further due to inflation.

Meanwhile, physical gold demand across major Asian markets remained weak this week, as volatility in rates deterred potential buyers from making purchases.

FUNDAMENTALS INTACT

However, the fundamentals for gold remained intact and "factors such as slowing economic growth, expensive equity market valuation, geopolitical uncertainty, and diversification away from U.S. assets are likely to sustain robust investment demand and central-bank buying," ANZ said in a note.

"I do think we are at the floor for gold prices at the moment. Prices may temporary go a little bit lower, but in general the path will be higher over the coming months," WisdomTree's Shah said.

Elsewhere, spot silver slid 3.3% to $48.94 per ounce, platinum lost 1.3% to $1,491.36, and palladium dipped 2% to $1,350.50.

data.title: VinFast's quarterly revenue jumps on strong deliveries
data.description:
Vietnam's VinFast reported a sharp rise in third-quarter revenue on Friday, bolstered by strong sales of its electric cars and bikes, with the company riding on the growth to fuel expansion.

VinFast's third-quarter revenue rose around 47% to $718.6 million from a year ago.

data.title: Uzbekistan – Three shortlisted for Tashkent-Andijan PPP
data.description:
By Cristiana Sandeva

Three companies have been shortlisted after responding to a request for proposals for the 171km Tashkent-Andijan toll road public-private partnership, with the winner due to be announced in H1.

Uzbekistan's deputy minister of transport made the announcement on the third day of the country's annual PPP conference but did not disclose the names of the bidders.

The tender was launched in January with bids first due April, then postponed to May and then summer. Bids have been assessed by the Uzbek government and the World Bank, which is its financial partner on the project. Advisers to the government include Arup, PwC and CMS.

data.title: NFE: Letter of credit facility extended, converted to committed, and commitments reduced for liquidity support
data.description:
The Ninth Amendment to the ULCA extends the letter of credit facility to November 14, 2025, converts it to a committed facility, reduces aggregate commitments, and increases cash collateralization requirements. The amendment provides updated schedules for lenders and issuance caps, supporting the company's liquidity needs.

Original document: New Fortress Energy Inc. [NFE] SEC 10-Q Quarterly Report — Nov. 21 2025

Disclaimer

data.title: Original-Research: MPH Health Care AG (von First Berlin Equity Research GmbH): Kaufen
data.description:
Original-Research: MPH Health Care AG - from First Berlin Equity Research GmbH

21.11.2025 / 12:01 CET/CEST

Dissemination of a Research, transmitted by EQS News - a service of EQS Group.

The issuer is solely responsible for the content of this research. The result of this research does not constitute investment advice or an invitation to conclude certain stock exchange transactions.

Classification of First Berlin Equity Research GmbH to MPH Health Care AG

Company Name:MPH Health Care AG
ISIN:
 
Reason for the research:9M/25 KPIs
Recommendation:Kaufen
from:21.11.2025
Target price:€37
Target price on sight of:12 Monate
Last rating change:-
Analyst:Ellis Acklin
First Berlin Equity Research hat ein Research Update zu MPH Health Care AG (ISIN: ) veröffentlicht. Analyst Ellis Acklin bestätigt seine BUY-Empfehlung und bestätigt sein Kursziel von EUR 37,00.Zusammenfassung: Der NAV sank in den ersten neun Monaten um rund 30% auf €194 Mio., was in erster Linie auf den starken Wertverlust der im ersten Halbjahr 2025 verbuchten Beteiligung an CR Energy zurückzuführen ist. Im Septemberquartal war der NAV stabiler (-5%). Die Portfolio-Performance von MPH wird nun ausschließlich von der Kerninvestition in M1 Kliniken bestimmt, die weiterhin gute Quartalsergebnisse mit solidem Wachstum und Margensteigerungen vorweisen kann. Vorbehaltlich der behördlichen Genehmigung plant die Haemato AG (85% M1-Beteiligung) den Verkauf des Handelsgeschäfts ihrer Tochtergesellschaft Haemato Pharm GmbH. Wir gehen davon aus, dass die Anleger die stärkere Fokussierung von M1 auf sein erfolgreiches Injectables-Geschäft begrüßen werden, und sehen diese Umstrukturierung als positiven Katalysator für die M1-Aktie sowie als Steigerung des NAV für MPH. Wir behalten unsere Kaufempfehlung und unser Kursziel von €37 (Aufwärtspotenzial: 111%) bei. First Berlin Equity Research has published a research update on MPH Health Care AG (ISIN: ). Analyst Ellis Acklin reiterated his BUY rating and maintained his EUR 37.00 price target.Abstract: NAV fell some 30% during the first nine months to €194m owing, chiefly to the sharp decline in the value of the CR Energy participation booked in H1/25. For the September quarter, NAV was more stable (-5%). MPH's portfolio performance will now be shaped solely by the core investment in M1 Kliniken, which continues to stack good quarterly results featuring solid growth and margin uplift. Pending regulatory approval, Haemato AG (85% M1 stake) plans to sell the trading operations run by its Haemato Pharm GmbH subsidiary. We think investors will welcome M1's sharpened focus on its successful injectables business, and we see this shake up as a good catalyst for M1 shares as well as NAV uplift for MPH. We stick to our Buy rating and €37 target price (upside: 111%). Bezüglich der Pflichtangaben gem. §34b WpHG und des Haftungsausschlusses siehe die vollständige Analyse. You can download the research here: 93M1_GR-2025-11-21_EN Contact for questions: First Berlin Equity Research GmbHHerr Gaurav TiwariTel.: +49 (0)30 809 39 686web: www.firstberlin.comE-Mail: g.tiwari@firstberlin.com

The EQS Distribution Services include Regulatory Announcements, Financial/Corporate News and Press Releases.

data.title: Global coffee prices plunge after Trump removes tariffs on Brazil
data.description:
Global coffee prices plunged on Friday after U.S. President Donald Trump removed 40% tariffs on imports of Brazilian agricultural products including coffee and cocoa in the face of growing angst from American consumers about high food costs.

U.S. retail coffee prices rose an annual 40% in September, due in part to tariffs, and rising food prices are a major factor behind Trump's approval ratings falling to their lowest since his return to power, a Reuters/Ipsos poll found.

Trump's overnight tariff removal on Brazil follows a similar order announced last Friday to roll back duties on coffee and scores of other agricultural products from producing countries.

Top coffee grower Brazil supplies the U.S., the world's largest coffee consumer, with about a third of its beans.

At 1017 GMT, arabica coffee futures on the ICE exchange , used as a benchmark to price physical coffee around the world, were down 4.6% to $3.5925 per lb, having earlier plunged more than 6% to two-month lows.

Futures prices for robusta coffee beans , typically used in instant coffee rather than in the roast and ground blends where arabica dominates, were down 5% to $4,400 a metric ton, having earlier sunk 8%.

"(We) need the market to digest this. More downside? Maybe, but I do not believe we'll go below $3/lb. If anything I would be a buyer into whatever market dip comes from this news," said a Europe-based trader at a top global coffee trade house.

He explained that the arabica crop was still in deficit, exchange certified and industry stocks were low, the industry was short on supply and needed to buy, and there were still weather risks due to the La Nina phenomenon.
